title: Slow-cooker lentil chilli
subtitle: Red lentils and kidney beans in a spiced tomato sauce
excerpt: Red lentils and kidney beans slow-cooked with tinned tomatoes, onion, chilli powder, cumin, and smoked paprika for six hours. The lentils break down into the sauce, thickening it naturally. Serve over rice or with jacket potatoes.
sourceNotes: Lentil chilli became a standard British vegetarian option in the 1990s and remains one of the most cooked plant-based slow-cooker recipes. Red lentils are used rather than Puy or green because their tendency to dissolve is an advantage here — they thicken the sauce naturally without any flour or cornflour. The kidney beans add the texture that the dissolved lentils do not.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes extremely well for up to 3 months.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe. Lentil chilli is an ideal batch cook.

content.text: Red lentils need no soaking and can go in raw. They will dissolve into the sauce over six hours, which is the desired outcome — they thicken the chilli naturally and contribute body. The kidney beans provide the texture the dissolved lentils cannot. Rinse the kidney beans well before adding.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a pan over medium heat. Fry the {{onion}} for 4 minutes. Add the {{garlic}}, {{chilli-powder}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, and {{tomato-puree}} and stir for 1 minute. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{red-lentils}}, {{kidney-beans}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{stock-vegetable}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Cook on low for 6 hours, until the lentils have dissolved into the sauce and the chilli is thick. Taste and adjust seasoning — start with half the chilli powder and taste at the end before adding more.

items.symptom: Chilli too spicy for the household
items.cause: UK chilli powder is pure ground chilli, much hotter than US chili powder blend
items.fix: Start with 0.5 teaspoon and taste at the end. Stir in a spoonful of soured cream or yoghurt to cool the heat in individual bowls
Add 100 to 200 ml boiling water and stir. The correct consistency should be thick but spoonable
Kidney beans in a 6-hour slow cooker hold their shape well. If they are dissolving, reduce the cook time to 5 hours next time

content.text: Lentil chilli is one of the best batch-cook options in this collection: cheap, nutritious, and improved by freezing and reheating. It serves six comfortably and the ingredients cost very little. Serve over rice, with jacket potatoes, or with soured cream, grated cheddar, and tortilla chips on a relaxed evening.
